If "Batatas willdenowii" is indeed a synonym for Dioscorea villosa, then here's a paragraph about the plant:

Dioscorea villosa, commonly known as Wild Yam, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the yam family. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS), as well as providing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea villosa include diosgenin, a plant steroid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, as well as other compounds such as alkaloids and saponins. Dioscorea villosa is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to alleviate menstrual symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
